completion_summary: Reviewed 3 assigned laws: 1 already incorporated in the current title 5 framework, 1 needs a concise statutory-note treatment with no live XML marker located, and 1 is temporary appropriations text that should remain out of the U.S. Code.

laws.title: Public Law 28-205 | Inspector General Reform Act of 2024
laws.status_basis: The source text is a general and permanent Inspector General enactment, and the current title 5 snapshots already carry the CIGIE framework and related executive-department and legislative-branch references that the act relies on.

current_implementation.detail: The live title 5 material already contains the Council of the Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency framework and the related department references, so no separate rewrite was identified from the supplied source.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, purpose, and repealment language.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the framing and repeal language as concise note material only.
provisions.evidence: The source opens with a short title, purpose, and a repealment clause aimed at earlier Inspector General material.
provisions.notes: No separate operative Code rewrite is exposed by the current snapshot.

provisions.exact_change: Leave the existing CIGIE and related title 5 framework in place.
provisions.evidence: The title 5 snapshots already contain the Council of the Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency and the department references used by the bill.
provisions.notes: No additional live XML change is warranted from the reviewed source.

recommended_actions: Leave the current title 5 framework unchanged.
Retain only concise historical note material if a record entry is needed.

laws.title: Public Law 29-209 | Terms of Service Act
laws.status_basis: The source text is general and permanent, but no live U.S. Code note marker or operative integration was located in the available snapshots.

current_implementation.detail: I did not find a matching live XML note or codified text for this enactment in the available snapshots.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the framing clauses as note-only material.
provisions.evidence: The opening sections supply only title, severability, and effective-date language.
provisions.notes: No operative Code text is created by these clauses.

provisions.exact_change: Preserve the findings as concise note material only.
provisions.evidence: The source states findings about proposed bills and Roblox Terms of Service concerns.

provisions.text_summary: Restrictions on bills that would violate Roblox Terms of Service or Game Rules.
provisions.exact_change: Record the restriction language as note-only material.
provisions.evidence: The section bars proposed bills that would violate the stated rules and lists prohibited content categories.
provisions.notes: No safe positive-law rewrite target was identified from the available code snapshots.

provisions.exact_change: Keep the repercussions language as note-only material.
provisions.evidence: The source directs removal of prohibited bills and escalation to chamber leadership.

recommended_actions: Add a concise statutory note if a codification anchor is later identified.
Do not manufacture operative text from the policy language.
Keep the current record free of dump-style boilerplate.

laws.title: Public Law 31-229 | Fiscal Year 25-1
laws.status_basis: The source is a temporary appropriations and funding measure, and the classification record marks it as no_code_text.

current_implementation.detail: No permanent U.S. Code text is warranted for a temporary appropriations law.

provisions.exact_change: Keep the framing clauses as history only.
provisions.evidence: The opening provisions only supply the title and immediate-effect clause.
provisions.notes: No permanent Code text is created.

provisions.text_summary: Appropriations, availability of funds, and congressional pay adjustment language.
provisions.exact_change: Exclude the funding and temporary compensation language from permanent Code text.
provisions.evidence: The act appropriates funds, conditions availability, and suspends a pay adjustment for the fiscal year.

provisions.exact_change: Keep the agency funding directives out of the U.S. Code.
provisions.evidence: The remaining sections set fiscal-year amounts for defense, justice, transportation, commerce, and state/foreign operations.

recommended_actions: Do not create permanent Code text.
Retain only brief historical record material if needed.
